source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/04_제품별 인허가 진행 문서/07_Retrofit (HnX-R1)/★ FDA 510(k) - K252912/참고자료/참고 - Cybersecurity - 최종 정리/이경수 팀장님/정재민 프로님/2) Cybersecurity requirements (IEC 60601-4-5).xlsx

sourceSummary.oneLine: Spreadsheet mapping IEC 60601-4-5 cybersecurity requirements to HnX-R1 Detector applicability and mitigation notes.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports cybersecurity requirement traceability for FDA 510(k) K252912 reference materials.
keyPoints: The source lists IEC 60601-4-5 cybersecurity controls including identification and authentication, use control, system integrity, data confidentiality, threat response, and recovery functions.
Many user-account and user-interface controls are marked N/A for the Detector because it does not provide a user interface or user account/login functionality.

Detector controls marked applicable include session timeout/sleep behavior, timestamping, integrity checks, malware protection through firmware integrity verification, encrypted updates, secure algorithms, restricted ports, audit log access, DoS connection handling, backup/recovery, and disabling unnecessary functions.

concepts.name: User Identification and Authentication
concepts.evidence: every interface accessible by user must include functionality for identifying and authenicating users.

concepts.name: Account Management
concepts.evidence: medical devices must either have the capability to manage all accounts or utilize the account management functionality of a parent system.

concepts.name: Session Lock
concepts.evidence: initiate session locking to prevent futher access if the device is not used within a configured time

concepts.name: Audit Log Generation
concepts.evidence: The medical device must have the functionality to generate audit records related to security types

concepts.name: Integrity Checking of Communication
concepts.evidence: The medical device must have the functionality to protect the integrity of transmitted information.

concepts.name: Verification of Authenticity and Integrity of Updates
concepts.evidence: The medical device must verify the authenticity and integrity of software updates or upgrades before installation.

concepts.name: Use of Secure Encryption
concepts.evidence: When encryption is required, medical devices must use cryptographic security features recommended domestically or internationally

concepts.name: Prevention of Denial of Service
concepts.evidence: Medical devices must have the capability to maintain essential functions during DoS (Denial of Service) events.

evidence.claim: The document is a cybersecurity requirements spreadsheet associated with IEC 60601-4-5.
evidence.evidenceText: 2) Cybersecurity requirements (IEC 60601-4-5).xlsx

evidence.claim: The source is associated with FDA 510(k) K252912 reference materials.
User-accessible interfaces must include user identification and authentication functionality.
evidence.evidenceText: every interface accessible by user must include functionality for identifying and authenicating users.

evidence.claim: The Detector is marked N/A for user identification and authentication because it does not provide a user interface.
evidence.evidenceText: N/A 디텍터는 사용자 인터페이스를 제공하지 않습니다.

evidence.claim: Medical devices must manage accounts or use parent-system account management functionality.
evidence.evidenceText: medical devices must either have the capability to manage all accounts or utilize the account management functionality of a parent system.

evidence.claim: The Detector is marked N/A for account management because it does not provide user account management and login functionality.
evidence.evidenceText: N/A 디텍터는 사용자 계정 관리 및 로그인 기능을 제공하지 않습니다.

evidence.claim: Medical devices with authentication functionality must limit consecutive failed login attempts and deny access after the limit is reached.
evidence.evidenceText: Upon reaching the attempt limit, access must be denied for a predetermined period or until unlocked by an administrator.

evidence.claim: For session locking, the Detector mitigation notes describe entering sleep mode after a configurable period of no use.
evidence.evidenceText: Detector : 일정 시간(설정 가능) 사용이 없는 경우 연결 해제 후 슬립 모드 진입

evidence.claim: Audit records should include timestamp, source, category, type, event ID, and event outcome.
evidence.evidenceText: Each individual audit record must include details such as: Timestamp Source Category Type Event ID Event outcome

evidence.claim: The Detector records timestamps by receiving PC environment time during the connection establishment process.
evidence.evidenceText: 연결 수립 절차에서 PC환경의 시간을 전달받아 사용. 로그 기록시 타임스탬프 함께 기록.

evidence.claim: Medical devices must protect the integrity of transmitted information.
evidence.evidenceText: The medical device must have the functionality to protect the integrity of transmitted information.

evidence.claim: The Detector mitigation states that integrity verification is performed for transmitted and received data.
evidence.evidenceText: 송수신되는 데이터에 대해 무결성 검증을 수행함.

evidence.claim: The Detector rejects damaged firmware during the update process through an integrity check step.
evidence.evidenceText: 훼손된 펌웨어는 업데이트 과정 중 무결성 검사 단계에서 발견 및 거부 처리됨.

evidence.claim: Medical devices must verify authenticity and integrity of software updates or upgrades before installation.
evidence.evidenceText: The medical device must verify the authenticity and integrity of software updates or upgrades before installation.

evidence.claim: The Detector performs SHA-256 integrity verification on update files.
evidence.evidenceText: Detector : 업데이트 파일에 대한 무결성 검증(SHA-256) 시행

evidence.claim: When encryption is required, medical devices must use domestically or internationally recommended cryptographic security features and securely manage encryption keys.
evidence.evidenceText: When encryption is required, medical devices must use cryptographic security features recommended domestically or internationally, and the encryption keys used must be securely managed.

evidence.claim: The Detector mitigation notes identify AES, RSA, and SHA-256 security algorithms and randomly generated encryption keys.
evidence.evidenceText: AES, RSA, SHA-256 등의 보안 알고리즘 사용. 암호화 키는 매번 무작위로 생성됨.

evidence.claim: Medical devices must allow only authorized users to access audit logs in read-only mode.
evidence.evidenceText: Medical devices must allow only authorized users to access audit logs in read-only mode.

evidence.claim: The Detector mitigation notes say internal logs can be loaded only through a separate engineer-only tool.
evidence.evidenceText: 별도의 엔지니어 전용 툴을 통해서만 내부 로그를 불러올 수 있음.

evidence.claim: Medical devices must maintain essential functions during DoS events.
evidence.evidenceText: Medical devices must have the capability to maintain essential functions during DoS (Denial of Service) events.

evidence.claim: The Detector mitigation notes say connections are immediately terminated when they deviate from the proper connection procedure.
evidence.evidenceText: 연결 절차에서 적합한 절차에 어긋나는 경우 해당 연결은 즉시 종료됨.

reviewFlags.reason: Document date is not present in the source text or metadata.
Revision or version is not present in the source text or metadata.
FDA authority is inferred from source metadata/path rather than the spreadsheet body.
Source appears to be a spreadsheet export with mixed English/Korean text and some typographical/OCR-like errors such as 'authenicating', 'deive', and 'norticiation'.
